model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Duke is the overwhelming favorite as the tournament's top-ranked team in both KenPom and Torvik, boasting a 31-2 record and elite efficiency on both ends of the floor. Siena, the MAAC champion, faces a massive talent gap, ranking 187th in Torvik and 192nd in KenPom, with no credible path to exploit Duke's weaknesses. While Siena's recent form and championship experience provide some intangible value, Duke's superior size, athleticism, and defensive prowess make this a near-certain outcome.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Efficiency Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke is the consensus #1 team in the nation, while Siena is a mid-major champion with a significant talent deficit.
reasoningStep.evidence: Duke ranks 1st in KenPom and Torvik with an adjusted efficiency margin of +39.17 (KenPom) and elite offensive (127.9 adjO) and defensive (88.7 adjD) ratings. Siena ranks 187th in Torvik and 192nd in KenPom, with a negative efficiency margin (-2.11) and much weaker metrics on both ends.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Duke Blue Devils Scores, Stats and Highlights - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search 1 Duke Blue Devils 32-2 1st in ACC Home Schedule Statistics Roster Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge NEW: TC Eliminator 2025-26 Schedule Postseason vs 16 Siena 3/19 2:50 pm CBS Regular Season vs Texas W 75-60 vs W Carolina W 95-54 @ Army W 114-59 vs Indiana St W 100-62 vs 24 Kansas W 78-66 vs Niagara W 100-42 vs Howard W 93-56 vs 22 Arkansas W 80-71 vs 15 Florida W 67-66 @ 7 Michigan St W 66-60 vs Lipscomb W 97-73 vs 19 Texas Tech L 82-81 vs Georgia Tech W 85-79 @ Florida St W 91-87 @ 20 Louisville W 84-73 vs 24 SMU W 82-75 @ California W 71-56 @ Stanford W 80-50 vs Wake Forest W 90-69 vs 20 Louisville W 83-52 @ Virginia Tech W 72-58 vs Boston College W 67-49 @ 14 North Carolina L 71-68 @ Pitt W 70-54 vs 20 Clemson W 67-54 vs Syracuse W 101-64 vs 1 Michigan W 68-63 @ Notre Dame W 100-56 vs 11 Virginia W 77-51 @ NC State W 93-64 vs 17 North Carolina W 76-61 vs Florida St W 80-79 vs Clemson W 73-61 vs 10 Virginia W 74-70 Full Schedule Jaylen Waddle Trade Reactions + Titans GM Mike Borgonzi Peter offers his perspective and instant analysis on the Broncos-Dolphins trade that brings Jaylen Waddle to Denver and leaves question marks in Miami. Then, Peter details just how much there was to the A.J. Brown-to-the-Rams rumors from a week ago. After some careless, reckless, and completely absurd NCAA Tournament thoughts, Peter brings in Titans GM Mike Borgonzi. They talk about the Titans' recent spending spree, which included a slew of new players and faces, and 17 additions to the roster. They go through what Robert Saleh and Brian Daboll are looking for in players and why so many former Jets and Giants were acquired in recent days. It's Cam Ward storyline, after that, with Borgonzi detailing what he's seen in their franchise QB over the course of the last eleven months. Then, Borgonzi goes down memory lane and takes us through his Kansas City days and what he remembers from scouting Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices 14h Blue Devils NCAAM No. 16 Siena vs. No. 1 Duke game preview 14h Blue Devils NCAAM Hour 2: Overreaction Tuesday, plus ESPN’s Jay Bilas Previews the NCAA Tournament ESPN College Basketball Analyst Jay Bilas and Rich preview the 2026 NCAA Tournament including his prediction of Duke and Michigan losing in upsets, the Arizona Wildcats winning it all, why Arkansas’ Darius Acuff Jr. could steal the spotlight and take the Razorbacks on a deep run, reveals that he thinks America hates Michigan basketball because of Rich’ non-stop honking, and says why the pairing of Charles Barkley and Dick Vitale might be the greatest thing to happen to sportscasting in decades. Rich and Brockman debate which teams are likely

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-1
Current game label: East 1/16
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs Siena (siena)
Prior committed picks: 4
